lady_seraphine_voss:idle.greeting: Lady Seraphine sets down her quill and inclines her head. "You come at an awkward hour. The court is half-empty and the rest are listening."

nodes.npcText: "You're not Thornwood-born — I would know. Tell me what brings you up the hill."

playerOptions.text: I want to understand who actually runs this keep.
I came to ask about your correspondence with Iyatte of Sandrun.

nodes.npcText: "I do — and Ser Corin keeps me alive while I do. The names that matter aren't on the rolls. I won't be one of them in front of you yet."

playerOptions.text: Then I'll leave you to it.

nodes.npcText: "You should not know that word, and yet you spoke it." She does not blink. "Whom have you also told?"

"Good. Continue not telling." She pulls a small lacquered box from a drawer; the latch is brass. "Bring this to the Verge crossroads at sunset. Don't open it. There will be a woman in red waiting. She'll know."
She smiles, which is worse than her not smiling. "Then we have nothing further to discuss. Ser Corin will show you out."
"The hill takes its time with newcomers. Return when you have something I don't."

apothecary_lyra_thorne:idle.greeting: Lyra is grinding something pale into a paste. She does not look up. "Take a seat or take a herb. Don't take both."

nodes.npcText: "You have the road-pallor. Bruise, fever, or something you'd rather not name?"

playerOptions.text: A bruise. Stiff knee from the ride up.
A question. I heard you trade cuttings cross-world.
Nothing — I was just curious about the shop.

nodes.npcText: "Comfrey wash, twice a day. Twenty sparks for the jar." She wraps it in waxed paper and slides it across. "And take an extra strip for the next bruise. There's always a next bruise."
Her hands still. "You heard wrong, or you heard from someone we share. Which?"

playerOptions.text: From Pia Thalis in the hub.

nodes.npcText: "Pia sends moonleaf seedlings every spring. I send her wormwood and lattice-root. Don't say any of that out loud. The infirmary is bigger than either of us."
"Come back when you're hurt enough to be honest."

witch_nymeria:idle.greeting: Nymeria is barefoot in two inches of bog water, humming. She turns when you arrive but does not move. "Walker. The mud will tell me what you want; you only have to wait."

nodes.npcText: "The bog has three layers tonight: water, peat, and lattice. You touched one already. Which?"

playerOptions.text: I'd like to cross to the Crucible.
I want to learn what you see in the bog.
I was sent by Father Galen.

nodes.npcText: "Then bring me a lattice-fragment. Any size. I'll teach you the eight steps, three of which I make up each time so the bog stays interested."
"You see what you have already brought with you. The bog is a mirror that lies politely. Robin sees colours; I see weight. What you'll see is yours."

playerOptions.text: Will you teach me to see?

nodes.npcText: She steps closer. Her feet make no sound in the water. "Bring me four nights' sleep saved up and one thing you regret. We'll start there."
"Galen still prays for me. That's kind of him. Tell him the bog does not need his prayers, but I read them anyway."
"The path back is the path in. Don't change your mind once you've stepped on it."

knight_corin_hale:idle.greeting: Ser Corin Hale stands at parade rest in the corridor, blue cloak folded. "Walker. Lady Seraphine is in audience. Speak, and I'll relay."

nodes.npcText: "Or you came to me. Which is it?"

playerOptions.text: I came to you. About the heir.

nodes.npcText: His shoulders do not move. "I have not heard you say a word. Continue not saying it. Then leave, and don't return to me about it."
"After my watch. The yard, fifth-bell. Bring a blade you trust. I don't fight to second blood; I fight until you learn one thing."
He nods once. "Walk on."
